title: toxicodendron coriaceum
intro: Toxicodendron coriaceum, also known as the poison oak of the Pacific, is a plant species belonging to the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its shiny leaves and reddish stems. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic compound that causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. Despite its toxic properties, Toxicodendron coriaceum has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial for treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tinctures and infusions, can be used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
